title: I Reason, Earth Is Short,
content:
I reason, earth is short,
    And anguish absolute,
    And many hurt;
    But what of that?

    I reason, we could die:
    The best vitality
    Cannot excel decay;
    But what of that?

    I reason that in heaven
    Somehow, it will be even,
    Some new equation given;
    But what of that?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "I Reason, Earth Is Short,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
